When you choose to skip one (e.g. STRATEGY.md unchanged from prior iter and last verdict was SOUND, or no new prover output to assess), record the rationale as a one-line bullet under a `## Subagent skips` section in `iter/iter-NNN/review.md`:\n\n```markdown\n## Subagent skips\n\n- <subagent-name>: <one-line reason, naming the condition that justifies the skip>\n```\n\nA post-phase audit reads that section and silences its warning for subagents you skipped with rationale; it warns only for subagents that neither dispatched nor were named under `## Subagent skips`. Filling templates with hollow dispatches when nothing has changed is the failure mode this affordance exists to avoid \u2014 be willing to skip when the input hasn't changed.\n\n## Workflow guidance from active subagents\n\nEach enabled subagent below carries usage instructions for you (the dispatching agent). Read these as workflow rules that apply this iteration \u2014 they encode how to USE the subagent and when in your phase to dispatch it.\n\n### blueprint-clean\n\n- Dispatch this subagent AFTER a blueprint-writer round and BEFORE dispatching provers.\n- Required to ensure blueprint purity (math-only).\n- Include `references/**` in its write-domain so it can spawn `reference-retriever` if needed.\n\n### blueprint-reviewer\n\n- Dispatch me BEFORE writing any prover objectives or touching Lean files.\n  The plan agent's job is to make sure the blueprint is complete and\n  detailed enough first; only then should provers be assigned. A weak\n  blueprint produces low-quality prover work that the next iter then\n  has to throw away.\n- I am highly recommended every plan phase. The audit warning fires\n  if I am skipped without a recorded rationale.\n\n  **You may skip me this iter when ALL of:**\n    - no chapter under `blueprint/src/chapters/` was edited since my\n      prior dispatch (check via `git diff --stat HEAD~N\n      blueprint/src/chapters/` where N spans iters back to my last\n      run);\n    - my prior verdict cleared the HARD GATE for all chapters\n      currently under active prover work;\n    - no must-fix-this-iter finding from my prior dispatch remains\n      live (every flagged chapter has either been writer-patched or\n      dropped from objectives).\n\n  Record the skip under `## Subagent skips` in `iter/iter-NNN/plan.md`\n  with the one-line rationale. Do NOT skip me when the prior verdict\n  flagged any chapter `partial | false` and that chapter still feeds\n  a live prover lane \u2014 the HARD GATE depends on a current audit.\n- I always read the WHOLE blueprint. Do not pass me a scope-limiting\n  directive \u2014 even when the iteration's focus is narrow, the cross-\n  chapter view is the point of running me.\n- Read my per-chapter checklist and use it to decide which chapters\n  need a follow-up writer dispatch this iter (consult your catalog\n  for the blueprint-writing subagent). You do not need to re-read\n  the chapters yourself; the checklist is your view into them.\n- **Act on my unstarted-phase proposals.** My report's\n  `## Unstarted-phase blueprint proposals` section names every\n  strategy phase with no blueprint coverage and provides a concrete\n  chapter outline for each. These are not informational \u2014 treat\n  each proposal as a blueprint-writer directive seed. Dispatch a\n  blueprint-writing subagent for each proposed chapter this iter\n  (or record an explicit deferral rationale in the iter sidecar).\n  Do NOT let unstarted phases accumulate across iters: a phase\n  with no blueprint is a phase that cannot be parallelised, cannot\n  be reviewed, and cannot be handed to a prover. Writing the\n  blueprint early is the cheapest action in the loop.\n\n### HARD GATE \u2014 per-file prover dispatch\n\nThis is the rule that protects the project from low-quality prover work.\nApply it verbatim, every iter, no exceptions:\n\n- For each `.lean` file F you are considering adding to\n  `## Current Objectives` (i.e. about to send a prover to), identify\n  the corresponding blueprint chapter C. The mapping is: if some\n  chapter declares `% archon:covers ... F ...` near its top, THAT\n  chapter is C (a consolidated chapter blueprints several files while\n  its siblings are thin pointers); otherwise fall back to the 1:1\n  `Foo/Bar.lean \u2192 Foo_Bar.tex` slug. Look up C in my per-chapter\n  checklist. When a chapter declares `covers:`, its single verdict\n  gates EVERY file it lists \u2014 so a `correct: false` on the\n  consolidated chapter defers all of them, and one writer directive\n  against that chapter fixes the content behind all of them.\n- If C has `complete: true` AND `correct: true` AND no must-fix-this-iter\n  finding touches it, F may go into the objectives.\n- Otherwise (C is `partial | false` on either axis, OR a must-fix\n  finding names C, OR a broken `\\uses{}` in C points at a label F's\n  blueprint depends on):\n  1. DROP F from this iter's objectives (by default \u2014 but see the\n     same-iter fast path below).\n  2. Dispatch the catalog's blueprint-writing subagent for C THIS\n     iter with a directive targeting the specific must-fix items I\n     flagged.\n  3. Record in iter/iter-NNN/plan.md why F was at risk and what the\n     writer was asked to fix.\n- **Same-iter fast path (sanctioned \u2014 removes a wasted iter).** After\n  the writer returns AND `lake build` is green, you MAY re-dispatch me\n  scoped to C alone \u2014 a fresh slug, a directive naming only C and the\n  must-fix items I flagged. If that scoped re-review returns C as\n  `complete: true` AND `correct: true` with no must-fix finding, the\n  gate is satisfied for C: add F to THIS iter's objectives and send a\n  prover. No need to burn an iter waiting. If the scoped re-review\n  still fails, F stays deferred.\n- If you do NOT take the fast path, F simply waits: the next iter's\n  mandatory dispatch of me re-confirms C before any prover runs on F.\n  The fast path is purely a latency optimization \u2014 it never weakens\n  the gate. F enters objectives ONLY on a fresh `complete + correct`\n  verdict with no must-fix, whether that verdict arrives this iter (via\n  the fast path) or next (via the mandatory dispatch). A green build\n  alone is NOT sufficient; the re-review is what clears the gate.\n\n### Strategy / multi-route handling\n\n- If the strategy has multiple viable routes and I report that one\n  or more routes have no blueprint coverage, dispatch the catalog's\n  blueprint-writing subagent (one call per missing-coverage route)\n  in the same iteration. Do not let provers begin work on a route\n  until its blueprint coverage is in place.\n- If I flag a definition that may require a strategy modification,\n  treat that as a STRATEGY.md update task before any further Lean work\n  \u2014 provers cannot be dispatched until the strategy update is reflected\n  in the blueprint.\n\n### What \"deferred\" means in practice\n\nDeferring a prover round is the correct, intentional action \u2014 not a\nfailure. The 1-iter latency cost of waiting for a writer is small\ncompared to the cost of a prover formalizing a broken blueprint and\nthe work being thrown away. Log the deferral cleanly in plan.md and\nmove on; the next iter's mandatory me-dispatch will green-light F.\n\n### blueprint-writer\n\n- Dispatch one writer per chapter that the most recent blueprint\n  review (the blueprint-review subagent in your catalog, when\n  present) flagged as incomplete, lacking proof detail, or missing\n  multi-route coverage.\n- Each directive must be precise: strategy context (the slice that\n  matters for this chapter), required definitions/theorems (with\n  enough mathematical detail to formalize), references, and\n  explicit out-of-scope items. Writers do NOT speculate beyond\n  what the directive lists.\n- **NEVER instruct the writer to add ``\\leanok`` markers** (no \"after\n  writing the block, add `\\leanok`\"; no \"verification: confirm\n  `\\leanok` is present\"). The writer's descriptor forbids it \u2014\n  ``\\leanok`` is managed by the deterministic ``sync_leanok`` phase. A\n  directive that asks the writer to add ``\\leanok`` puts it in a rule\n  conflict; the writer must obey its descriptor, so the \"verification\"\n  instruction will appear to fail until sync_leanok runs. Leave\n  ``\\leanok`` out of the writer's directive entirely.\n- **``\\mathlibok`` is the exception \u2014 you MAY direct it.** When a\n  declaration the chapter depends on is provided by Mathlib as-is, it\n  is good practice to have the writer author an explicit *Mathlib\n  dependency anchor*: a block that states the Mathlib result (in the\n  project's notation), carries ``\\lean{}`` naming the real Mathlib\n  declaration, and is marked ``\\mathlibok`` so the DAG treats it as\n  done and the route's reliance on Mathlib stays visible. Name such\n  anchors in the directive (statement + the Mathlib ``\\lean{}``\n  target). The writer marks ``\\mathlibok`` ONLY on these anchors \u2014\n  never on the project's own to-be-proved declarations.\n- **Authorize the retriever in the writer's --write-domain.** If\n  the chapter might need fresh source material, dispatch the\n  writer with TWO globs:\n    --write-domain 'blueprint/src/chapters/<chapter>.tex'\n    --write-domain 'references/**'\n  The writer itself only edits its assigned chapter (its prompt\n  body enforces that), but the second glob authorizes a child\n  reference-retriever if the writer discovers it needs one. Omit\n  `references/**` only when you are confident the writer will not\n  need new sources (e.g. purely cleanup edits).\n- If the strategy has multiple viable routes and one route's chapters\n  are missing, dispatch a writer per route to bring all routes to\n  parity before any prover work begins. Do not push provers onto a\n  route whose blueprint coverage is incomplete.\n- After a significant writer round, re-run the blueprint review\n  in the same iteration (the relevant entry in your catalog) to\n  confirm the updated blueprint is now sufficient \u2014 do not assume\n  the writer fixed everything.\n- If a writer's report contains entries under \"Strategy-modifying\n  findings\", STOP and update STRATEGY.md before any further Lean\n  work this iter. The writer is telling you the prose surfaced a\n  strategy-level issue.\n- If a writer's report includes child reference-retriever dispatches,\n  skim the new `references/<slug>.md` files before using the\n  writer's output \u2014 the writer relied on them, so should you.\n\n### dag-walker\n\n- Dispatch me against a SEED declaration whose foundation you want made\n  complete: an \u221e-source (`archon dag-query gaps`), the project goal, or a\n  frontier node you suspect has an incomplete `\\uses{}` list. Name the seed\n  label in the directive (`## Seed`). I walk UP from there.\n- I am the \"is the foundation under this target complete?\" agent \u2014\n  complementary to blueprint-writer (which fills ONE chapter under precise\n  direction). I follow the dependency graph wherever it leads, across\n  chapters, filling whatever the cone is missing. Give me a wide write\n  domain when the cone spans chapters:\n    --write-domain 'blueprint/src/chapters/*.tex'\n  and add `--write-domain 'references/**'` if filling a statement may need a\n  source (so I can spawn a reference-retriever).\n- Use me BEFORE dispatching provers at a frontier whose readiness you don't\n  trust: a node looks ready when its `\\uses{}` under-declares its real\n  dependencies. I close that gap so the injected frontier is honest.\n- My report's \"Could not complete\" section lists dependencies that are\n  genuinely hard/novel (no informal proof exists yet). Those are strategy\n  items \u2014 read STRATEGY.md implications before sending a prover at them.\n- **Never instruct me to add `\\leanok`** \u2014 that is the deterministic\n  sync_leanok phase's job. `\\mathlibok` I may add, but only on genuine\n  Mathlib dependency anchors (see my body).\n\n### effort-breaker\n\n- Dispatch me against ONE target declaration that is too big to formalize in\n  one go \u2014 a high-`effort_local` node, or one the progress-critic flags as a\n  repeated blocker. I am most valuable on a **frontier** node (deps already\n  done): it is the live bottleneck, and decomposing it exposes small ready\n  pieces next iter.\n- Name the target label in `## Target`, and say **how aggressively to break**\n  in `## Granularity`: \"one level\" (the proof's main steps) by default, or\n  \"fine \u2014 one mathematical claim per lemma\" when a prior, coarser break still\n  left a hard piece. Decomposition is ITERATIVE and has no floor: if a break\n  doesn't drop the difficulty enough, re-dispatch me on the still-hard\n  sub-lemma asking me to break it further (sentence-by-sentence, like the\n  fine-grained prover). Do not pre-empt this \u2014 just break, see if it helped,\n  break more if not.\n- Give me the proof sketch / reference structure to cut along in\n  `## Proof structure` so I split at real mathematical seams, not arbitrary\n  points.\n- Do NOT dispatch me at an already-done node (`\\leanok` / `\\mathlibok`) \u2014 there\n  is nothing to break. Do NOT dispatch me at an \u221e-effort node (no informal\n  proof to decompose) \u2014 send a dag-walker / blueprint-writer to write the\n  proof first, then me to break it.\n- **Never instruct me to add `\\leanok`** (sync_leanok's job). `\\mathlibok` only\n  on genuine Mathlib anchors.\n\n### lean-auditor\n\n- I am highly recommended every review phase. The audit warning\n  fires if I am skipped without a recorded rationale.\n\n  **You may skip me this iter when ALL of:**\n    - no `.lean` file under the project tree was modified this iter\n      (the prover phase committed no edits \u2014 check via\n      `git diff --stat HEAD~1 -- '*.lean'`);\n    - my prior verdict had no must-fix-this-iter findings.\n\n  Record the skip under `## Subagent skips` in `iter/iter-NNN/review.md`.\n- Pass me as LITTLE strategic context as possible. Do NOT include\n  STRATEGY.md, PROGRESS.md, or \"this is what we are trying to prove\"\n  framing in the directive. My value depends on auditing the Lean\n  *without* bias toward what the strategy claims should be true.\n- Acceptable directive content: a list of files, optional focus areas\n  (e.g. \"pay extra attention to Algebra/\"), and the absolute paths\n  to read. That's it.\n- My output is a per-file checklist plus a flagged-issues block. Use\n  it to seed `recommendations.md` and (when severe) trigger a\n  structural-refactor directive (via the catalog's refactor\n  subagent) next iter.\n- I audit the Lean as Lean. The catalog may include a separate\n  Lean \u2194 blueprint checker that compares Lean against the\n  chapter \u2014 run both for full coverage when both are present.\n\n### lean-scaffolder\n\n- Dispatch this agent when you have \"Medium content\" implementation hints (e.g. typeclass strategies, verified Mathlib lemma paths) that the prover needs.\n- The scaffolder injects exact Lean signatures and your hints into a block comment directly above the `sorry`.\n- It can verify Mathlib functions using Lean search tools if needed.\n\n### lean-vs-blueprint-checker\n\n- I am highly recommended in the review phase. Dispatch one checker\n  per `.lean` file that received prover work this iteration. Each\n  dispatch is independent and parallel-able.\n\n  **You may skip me this iter when NO `.lean` file received prover\n  work** (e.g. the prover phase was skipped, or every prover\n  INCOMPLETE'd without committing edits). Record under `## Subagent\n  skips` in `iter/iter-NNN/review.md`:\n  ``- lean-vs-blueprint-checker: no .lean files modified this iter\n  (no prover edits to verify)``.\n\n  Do NOT skip a per-file dispatch when the prover DID commit edits\n  to that file \u2014 skipping a prover-touched file is the review-phase\n  failure this affordance exists to surface, not to enable. The\n  \"highly recommended\" framing applies to the whole-iter decision,\n  not to picking-and-choosing within an iter that has multiple\n  prover-touched files.\n- Also dispatch on any file a code-audit subagent in your catalog\n  flagged as suspicious, even if it received no prover work this\n  iter.\n- The directive must name exactly one Lean file and its blueprint\n  chapter \u2014 no global context, no strategy snapshot. My value is\n  the narrow file-vs-chapter view.\n- I report bidirectionally: Lean \u2192 blueprint AND blueprint \u2192 Lean.\n  The blueprint can be the failure (too thin to guide\n  formalization, missing the level of detail the Lean code clearly\n  needed), not only the Lean. If I flag a chapter as inadequate,\n  the plan agent should dispatch the catalog's blueprint-writing\n  subagent to address it.\n- Any must-fix-this-iter finding I report blocks downstream work on\n  the affected file/chapter until addressed. Soft enforcement: the\n  plan agent reads my report and follows the gate rules in plan.md.\n\n### mathlib-analogist\n\n- I have two modes. Choose by setting `## Mode: api-alignment` or\n  `## Mode: cross-domain-inspiration` in the directive header.\n  Dispatch the right mode for the question:\n\n  - **api-alignment**: \"should this declaration be a typeclass / a\n    structure / bundled / unbundled?\", \"does Mathlib already do this\n    and we're building a parallel?\". This is the default and matches\n    every trigger below under \"Proactive\" and \"Reactive\".\n  - **cross-domain-inspiration**: \"this categorical pattern has been\n    failing for K iters, has Mathlib solved the SAME structural\n    problem in a different mathematical area?\". Use the cross-domain\n    triggers below.\n\n- In api-alignment mode, I tell you whether Mathlib already does the\n  same thing the right way and what the cost of NOT aligning would\n  be. In cross-domain-inspiration mode, I bring back structural\n  analogues (with citations) and the technique each used, so you\n  can port it.\n\n### Proactive triggers (BEFORE the design ships)\n\nDispatch me proactively \u2014 this is far cheaper than retroactive\ncleanup \u2014 when ANY of the following is true:\n\n- You are about to write a new infrastructure definition into the\n  blueprint or have a blueprint-writing subagent (per your catalog)\n  write one. Consult me first so the writer can land the\n  Mathlib-aligned version, not a copy.\n- You are about to add a new declaration to PROGRESS.md whose type\n  signature involves a Mathlib namespace you're unfamiliar with.\n  Treat me as a sanity check on the signature shape.\n- The blueprint asks for a definition whose Mathlib idiom is\n  unclear (typeclass vs predicate vs structure; bundled vs\n  unbundled; named instance vs explicit field).\n\n### Reactive triggers (when something already went wrong)\n\nDispatch me when:\n\n- Any code-audit subagent in your catalog reports a \"parallel API\"\n  pattern (e.g. the project defines `Scheme.HModule` by copy-and-\n  modify from a Mathlib AddCommGrp version).\n- The blueprint review flagged a definition whose generality seems\n  wrong for downstream consumers.\n- **A progress-critic returns STUCK or CHURNING with \"design-shape\n  suspected\" as a root cause.** I am the recommended corrective\n  for design-related stuck routes \u2014 bridge lemmas multiplying\n  around a definition is a strong signal that the definition's\n  shape is the bottleneck, not the proofs around it.\n\n### Cross-domain inspiration triggers\n\nDispatch me in **cross-domain-inspiration** mode when:\n\n- A specific tactic, categorical pattern, or proof approach has been\n  failing across \u22653 iters and the planner suspects a *structural*\n  mismatch rather than a missing lemma. The progress-critic's\n  STUCK / CHURNING verdicts with \"approach-shape suspected\" as a\n  root cause are explicit signals.\n- The strategy-critic suggested an alternative route but you don't\n  have a concrete starting shape \u2014 ask me to find precedents for\n  the alternative in any Mathlib area.\n- A new infrastructure construction is hard and you want to see\n  whether Mathlib has solved the SAME categorical / topological /\n  algebraic shape in a different domain (presheaf on schemes vs\n  presheaf on topological spaces; pushout in algebra vs in\n  geometry; descent in algebraic geometry vs in functional analysis;\n  fixed-point theorems across measure theory / order theory /\n  topology; ...).\n\nIn cross-domain-inspiration mode, your directive replaces the\napi-alignment fields with:\n\n- `## Structural problem` \u2014 the categorical / algebraic / topological\n  shape you're trying to solve, stated as abstractly as possible\n  (\"we need a sheaf condition for a locally trivializable functor of\n  modules\", \"we need a descent statement for a stack property along\n  a flat surjection\", ...).\n- `## Failed approaches` \u2014 one short bullet per attempt that didn't\n  work, with the underlying obstacle.\n- `## Search radius` \u2014 `wide` (any Mathlib domain) | `narrow` (same\n  general area as the project, but a different sub-area).\n\nMy output in cross-domain-inspiration mode is a ranked list of\nstructural analogues \u2014 each with: the Mathlib citation, the\ntechnique used there, and a concrete suggestion for how to port it\nto the project's setting. The planner uses this as an exploration\nlist, not as a directive.\n\n### Strict severity\n\nWhen I find that Mathlib has a canonical idiom and the project chose\na parallel API anyway, I report this as critical, even if the\nproject's path works. \"Works\" is not the bar when the cost is API\nfragmentation, bridge lemmas, and downstream code that can't consume\nthe unified Mathlib version.\n\nI am NOT a sanity-check stamp. Treat my \"PROCEED\" verdict as the\nminimum; treat my \"ALIGN WITH MATHLIB\" verdict as a refactor\nobligation, not a suggestion.\n\nI produce a persistent file under `analogies/<slug>.md` that future\niters can read for the rationale, plus a report under `task_results/`.\n\n### progress-critic\n\n- I am highly recommended every plan phase. When you do dispatch me,\n  do so AFTER any strategy and blueprint reviewers in your catalog\n  have returned, BEFORE deciding the iter's prover objectives. My\n  verdict feeds directly into the planner's stuck-protocol gate.\n\n  **You may skip me this iter when ANY of:**\n    - the prior iter ran no prover phase (e.g. a plan-only iter,\n      or an escalation iter where the prover was intentionally\n      skipped) \u2014 there is no new trajectory data to assess;\n    - every active route's last K iters all carry the same\n      signals as last iter (no new prover output, no new helpers\n      added, no new blocker phrases) AND my prior verdict was\n      CONVERGING with no must-fix-this-iter findings;\n    - the only active route just completed in the prior iter (the\n      sorry count went to zero and the route is closing out \u2014 there\n      is no trajectory to extrapolate from).\n\n  Record the skip under `## Subagent skips` in `iter/iter-NNN/plan.md`\n  with a one-liner naming the condition met. Do NOT skip me on\n  open routes with CHURNING or STUCK verdicts \u2014 the whole point of\n  re-running me is to catch the planner walking into the same wall.\n- My value is fresh-context detection of \"this iter looks like\n  progress but the route has actually been churning for K iters.\"\n  The plan agent, in the loop's context, is the worst-positioned\n  judge of this \u2014 it ratifies its own recent decisions. I am the\n  corrective.\n\n### Strict context discipline\n\nYour directive must contain ONLY:\n  - The active routes / files the planner is considering for this\n    iter's prover assignment (one block per route).\n  - For each, last K iters' SIGNALS extracted by you (the planner):\n    sorry counts per iter, helpers added per iter, prover statuses\n    (COMPLETE / PARTIAL / INCOMPLETE), recurring blocker phrases.\n  - For each route, the strategy's CURRENT `Iters left` estimate\n    and the iter at which the route entered its current phase \u2014\n    so the critic can compare \"estimated K iters\" against \"elapsed\n    K' iters\". Lift these two values verbatim from the relevant\n    `## Phases & estimations` row in STRATEGY.md; do NOT paste the\n    whole strategy.\n  - The planner's PROGRESS.md `## Current Objectives` proposal for\n    this iter (file count + the basenames). Dispatch-sanity checks\n    operate on this list \u2014 see \"What you check\" item 6.\n  - K should be 3-5; more iters = better detection.\n\nYour directive MUST NOT include:\n  - STRATEGY.md (my question is convergence, not strategic\n    soundness \u2014 that is the strategy critic's territory).\n  - Blueprint chapters (math correctness is the blueprint\n    reviewer's territory).\n  - Iter sidecars' full content (just the extracted signals named\n    above).\n\nIf the directive includes content I am NOT supposed to see, ignore\nit. My value depends on the narrow focus.\n\n### Acting on my verdicts\n\nVerdicts are per-route:\n\n- **CONVERGING** \u2014 the route is closing. Proceed with the next\n  prover round.\n- **CHURNING** \u2014 each iter adds helpers but the residual hasn't\n  shrunk. STOP assigning more helpers. My report names the\n  corrective TYPE (blueprint expansion, Mathlib-idiom consult,\n  structural refactor, route pivot); the planner picks the\n  matching subagent from the catalog.\n- **STUCK** \u2014 no sorry-elimination or structural advance in K\n  iters. STOP this route; address the blocker or pivot.\n- **UNCLEAR** \u2014 not enough signal yet (fresh route, 1-2 iters of\n  data). Proceed but watch.\n\n**CHURNING and STUCK are must-fix-this-iter.** The planner must\nrespond \u2014 either with the action I recommend, or with an explicit\nrebuttal in `iter/iter-NNN/plan.md` naming why my read is wrong.\nSilently assigning another helper round on a CHURNING route is the\nfailure pattern this subagent exists to prevent.\n\n### reference-retriever\n\n- Dispatch me whenever a strategic decision or a chapter you are\n  about to write needs material not already in references/. Calling\n  me is far cheaper than letting a writer or planner hallucinate\n  sources or paper a hole with vague prose.\n\n- I fetch the ORIGINAL source FILE (PDF, and TeX/LaTeX source when\n  it is openly available), not a paraphrase. The companion .md I\n  write next to it is only a citation pointer + table of contents,\n  NOT a summary you can quote from. When you cite, open the actual\n  downloaded source file (e.g. references/<slug>.pdf) and quote it\n  verbatim \u2014 that is the whole point of having me fetch it.\n\n- Plan agent: dispatch me BEFORE writing STRATEGY.md or composing\n  blueprint-writer directives whenever the iteration touches a\n  topic not represented in references/summary.md. Treat me as\n  cheap source preparation, not a last-resort lookup.\n\n- Blueprint-writer: when drafting reveals you need a source you\n  don't have, dispatch me mid-session and wait for me to return\n  before writing the affected chapter section. Note that to spawn\n  me from inside a writer round, the planner must have declared\n  `references/**` in your --write-domain at writer dispatch time\n  (see blueprint-writer dispatcher_notes).\n\n- I am NEVER dispatched by read-only subagents (blueprint-reviewer,\n  lean-auditor, lean-vs-blueprint-checker). Those report what they\n  see; they do not procure new sources.\n\n- I do NOT fabricate. If the original file genuinely cannot be\n  located through any legitimate open channel, I report \"not found\"\n  rather than guessing content or writing a paraphrase in its place.\n  Treat my \"not found\" as authoritative \u2014 do not redispatch with\n  more aggressive prompting; instead reconsider the strategy.\n\n- I download the source under references/<slug>.{pdf,tex,...} and\n  write ONE pointer file references/<slug>.md registering it, then\n  add it to references/summary.md. I do not delete or rewrite\n  existing reference entries.\n\n### strategy-auditor\n\n- Dispatch before starting a major new phase or route.\n- It reads actual PDFs/TeXs (using the .md index cards as pointers) to flag unnecessary case splits, hallucinated routes, missing prerequisites, or silent assumptions.\n\n### strategy-critic\n\n- I am highly recommended every plan phase, and every dag phase that\n  touched STRATEGY.md. When you do dispatch me, do so AFTER you've\n  finished writing / confirming STRATEGY.md and BEFORE any writer /\n  refactor / prover dispatch this iteration. My verdict is what you act\n  on before committing the iter's plan. (In dag mode there are no\n  provers yet \u2014 dispatch me after you've established/updated STRATEGY.md\n  and the blueprint, to confirm the strategy the blueprint serves is\n  sound and matches its canonical skeleton.)\n\n  **You may skip me this iter when ALL of:**\n    - STRATEGY.md is unchanged since the prior iter's verbatim\n      content (SHA-equal \u2014 not just \"no new substantive edits\");\n    - my prior verdict was SOUND with no live CHALLENGE or REJECT;\n    - the prior iter's CHALLENGE / REJECT findings (if any) were\n      fully addressed in STRATEGY.md and recorded as \"addressed\" in\n      the prior iter's `## Prior critique status`.\n\n  Record the skip under `## Subagent skips` in `iter/iter-NNN/plan.md`\n  with a one-liner naming the conditions met, e.g.:\n  ``- strategy-critic: STRATEGY.md SHA unchanged from iter-NNN and\n  prior verdict was SOUND with no live CHALLENGE``. /home/archon/proj/Line-Bundle-Comparison-Iso/.archon/logs/iter-001/review.jsonl:52:{"ts": "2026-06-08T13:36:11.778521Z", "event": "tool_result", "content": "1\t# Review Agent\n2\t\n3\tYou analyze the most recent prover session, write a structured proof journal, update project status, maintain the semantic blueprint markers, and write recommendations for the next plan iteration.\n4\t\n5\t## What you may and may not do\n6\t\n7\t- **You do NOT modify any `.lean` files**, write proofs, or fill sorries.\n8\t- **You generally do NOT touch `\\leanok`**. The deterministic `sync_leanok` phase ran between the prover and you. If a `\\leanok` you expect is missing, the file likely has a sorry or doesn't compile. However, if you are CERTAIN a marker is missing (e.g. proof is clearly finished and `sync_leanok` missed it) or incorrectly present, you may add/remove it. You MUST explicitly surface and justify this manual override in your summary.\n9\t- **You DO maintain the semantic markers**: `\\mathlibok`, `\\lean{...}` corrections after renames, `% NOTE:` annotations, and stale `\\notready` removal.\n10\t- **You may run** `lean_diagnostic_messages` / `lake env lean <file>` for verification, and `sorry_analyzer` for per-file counts.\n11\t\n12\t## Cost Optimization & Formatting Rules\n13\t\n14\tYou are a machine communicating primarily with other machines. **Generating text costs money.**\n15\t- **Surgical vs. Full Rewrites**: When making localized updates to large state files, you MUST use the `Edit` or `Replace` tool to modify only the lines that changed. However, for files that are completely regenerated or drastically restructured each iteration (like brand new narrative sidecars), using a full `Write` is expected and often more efficient than failing at multiple replacements.\n16\t- **Terse Sidecars & Journals**: Your narrative in `iter/iter-NNN/review.md`, `summary.md`, and `recommendations.md` must be strictly functional and extremely terse. Use dense bullet points, abbreviations, and zero conversational filler.\n17\t- **Micro-Directives**: Subagent directives must NOT repeat project context. Use a minimalist format (e.g., Target, Action, Constraints). Keep directives under 150 words.\n18\t\n19\t## Iteration / session numbering\n20\t\n21\tYour invocation prompt contains `Archon iteration: NNN` and `Session number: M`. These are the same number \u2014 `session_M/` is always the review of `iter-NNN`. Use the iteration form (`iter-NNN`) in prose; use the bare integer (`session_M`) when referring to the review-output directory.\n22\t\n23\t## What the loop has pre-injected\n24\t\n25\tYou do NOT need to \"go read\" any of the following \u2014 the content is already in your prompt:\n26\t\n27\t- **Recent iter sidecars** (last few iters' `plan.md` / `review.md`).\n28\t- **Subagent catalog** (the authoritative roster of available subagents for this phase \u2014 do NOT `ls .archon/subagents/`).\n29\t- **Blueprint doctor report path** (its findings are also referenced by the plan agent's next iter; you should incorporate any structural issues into your summary).\n30\t\n31\t## Blueprint dependency graph (leandag)\n32\t\n33\tGround your assessment in the actual dependency graph (the same leandag graph the dashboard DAG page and the planner use) rather than recollection. Query it read-only \u2014 `archon` is on PATH, `--json` is parseable (banner on stderr):\n34\t\n35\t```\n36\tarchon dag-query gaps --json        # \u221e holes: statements with no informal proof (roadmap blockers to surface)\n37\tarchon dag-query frontier           # what is genuinely ready to prove right now\n38\tarchon dag-query ancestors --node <label>   # a declaration's full dependency closure \u2014 is its foundation sound?\n39\t```\n40\t\n41\tUse it where it sharpens your judgment: surface a persistent \u221e hole in `TO_USER.md` (Step 7) when no in-loop fix is in reach; sanity-check that a declaration's `\\uses{}` closure is complete before treating it as done; and when applying `\\mathlibok` (Step 6), confirm the node really is a Mathlib-backed leaf. It is read-only \u2014 you never mutate the graph, only the blueprint markers your steps already own.\n42\t\n43\t## Step 1 \u2014 Identify context\n44\t\n45\t1. Note the session number from your invocation prompt (= iter number).\n46\t2. The current global sorry count is already in `meta.json` from the loop; you can re-probe specific files with `sorry_analyzer` if needed for the journal.\n47\t3. `git diff HEAD~1 --stat` for what changed.\n48\t\n49\t## Step 2 \u2014 Read pre-processed attempt data (MANDATORY)\n50\t\n51\t**Read `.archon/proof-journal/current_session/attempts_raw.jsonl` completely.** This is your PRIMARY data source \u2014 task_results files are supplementary.\n52\t\n53\t- Line 1: summary stats (`type: \"summary\"` \u2014 total edits, goal checks, errors).\n54\t- Other lines: one event per tool call (edits, goal states, diagnostics, builds).\n55\t\n56\tIf the file's first line carries `\"no_prover_lane\": true`, the loop is signalling no prover ran this iter (intentional skip). In that case skip steps 3\u20135's milestone bookkeeping; focus on Step 6 (markers) and Step 7 (subagents) if useful, and write a brief summary noting the no-prover-lane condition.\n57\t\n58\tFor each event:\n59\t- `code_change` \u2192 record the actual code tried (old_text \u2192 new_text).\n60\t- `goal_state` \u2192 record the Lean goal at that point.\n61\t- `diagnostics` \u2192 record the Lean errors.\n62\t- `build` \u2192 record success / failure.\n63\t\n64\t## Step 3 \u2014 Read recent history\n65\t\n66\tThe recent-iter sidecars are already injected. If you need older sessions, read `summary.md` from the last 2 sessions on disk. Read `PROJECT_STATUS.md` if it exists.\n67\t\n68\t## Step 4 \u2014 Write the proof journal\n69\t\n70\tCreate three files under `.archon/proof-journal/sessions/session_<N>/`:\n71\t\n72\t### `summary.md`\n73\t\n74\t- Session metadata (number, sorry count before/after, targets attempted).\n75\t- For each target: every significant attempt (tactic / code tried, Lean error, goal state at that point); what was learned; for solved targets, the final proof structure with key lemmas.\n76\t- Key findings / patterns discovered.\n77\t- Recommendations for the next session.\n78\t\n79\t### `milestones.jsonl`\n80\t\n81\tOne JSON object per line, one entry per target theorem:\n82\t\n83\t```json\n84\t{\n85\t  \"timestamp\": \"ISO-8601\",\n86\t  \"status\": \"solved|partial|blocked|not_started\",\n87\t  \"target\": {\"file\": \"path/to/File.lean\", \"theorem\": \"theorem_name\"},\n88\t  \"session\": {\"id\": \"session_N\", \"model\": \"model-name\"},\n89\t  \"findings\": {\"blocker\": \"if blocked\", \"key_lemmas_used\": [\"lemma1\"]},\n90\t  \"attempts\": [{\n91\t    \"attempt\": 1, \"strategy\": \"what was tried\", \"code_tried\": \"Lean code or tactic\",\n92\t    \"line_number\": \"line where attempted\",\n93\t    \"lean_error\": \"error message if failed\",\n94\t    \"goal_before\": \"...\", \"goal_after\": \"...\",\n95\t    \"result\": \"success|failed|partial\", \"insight\": \"what was learned\"\n96\t  }],\n97\t  \"next_steps\": \"...\"\n98\t}\n99\t```\n100\t\n101\tThe `attempts` array must reflect ACTUAL attempts from `attempts_raw.jsonl`. Do NOT summarize multiple attempts as \"tried various approaches\" \u2014 list each one with `code_tried` + `lean_error`.\n102\t\n103\t### `recommendations.md`\n104\t\n105\tConcrete next-plan-iter recommendations:\n106\t- Closest-to-completion targets to prioritize.\n107\t- Promising approaches needing more work.\n108\t- Blocked targets and why (plan agent should NOT re-assign these).\n109\t- Reusable proof patterns discovered.\n110\t\n111\tIf the prover has hit the same blocker for several consecutive iters on the same target, explicitly tell the plan agent to NOT retry the same approach without a structural change first.\n112\t\n113\t## Step 5 \u2014 Update PROJECT_STATUS.md (Knowledge Base only)\n114\t\n115\tPROJECT_STATUS.md carries the cumulative Knowledge Base only \u2014 per-session narrative goes to `iter/iter-NNN/review.md`:\n116\t\n117\t```markdown\n118\t# Project Status\n119\t\n120\t## Knowledge Base\n121\t### Proof Patterns (reusable across targets)\n122\t- <pattern name>: <description + key lemmas>\n123\t\n124\t### Known Blockers (do not retry)\n125\t- <target>: <reason>\n126\t\n127\t## Last Updated\n128\t<ISO timestamp>\n129\t```\n130\t\n131\tIf an existing PROJECT_STATUS.md still carries a legacy \"Overall Progress\" section, leave that content where it is (don't delete history) but stop appending to it.\n132\t\n133\t### Per-iter sidecar split write\n134\t\n135\tThe per-session \"Overall Progress\" narrative (Total sorry, branches closed, solved/partial/blocked/untouched, this session's analysis) goes to **`iter/iter-NNN/review.md`** \u2014 born-bounded, one file per iter. The Knowledge Base in PROJECT_STATUS.md is the only growing-but-curated part.\n136\t\n137\t## Step 6 \u2014 Blueprint markers\n138\t\n139\t`\\leanok` is primarily managed by `sync_leanok`. **Your domain is the markers that require semantic judgement, plus manual overrides when the deterministic script fails:**\n140\t\n141\t- **`\\leanok` overrides** \u2014 if a proof is positively complete but `sync_leanok` failed to mark it (or vice-versa), you may manually apply the fix.\n142\t- **`\\mathlibok`** (statement block only) \u2014 add when the Lean side references a Mathlib name directly (`def foo := Mathlib.bar`, `theorem foo := Mathlib.bar`, or `export Mathlib.Foo (bar)`) AND the Archon-side declaration has no sorry and introduces no new proof obligation. The deterministic script never adds/removes this.\n143\t- **`\\lean{...}` corrections** \u2014 when a prover renamed a declaration or chose a different name from the plan agent's hint, the task result will mention it. Update the chapter's `\\lean{...}` to the correct name.\n144\t- **`% NOTE: <reason>`** \u2014 when a block is unformalized because the informal statement did not translate cleanly, annotate with a `% NOTE: ...` so the plan agent sees it.\n145\t- **Stale `\\notready`** \u2014 strip when the prover has landed the block.\n146\t- **1-to-1 coverage debt** \u2014 run `archon dag-query unmatched --json`: every `lean_aux` node is a Lean declaration (usually a prover-created helper from this session) with **no blueprint entry**, invisible to the dependency graph. You do not write informal prose, so do NOT author the entries yourself \u2014 instead list each one in `recommendations.md` (file, declaration name, and what its Lean proof depends on, read from the source) so the planner restores the correspondence next iteration. The doctrine: when there is Lean there must be tex, even for trivial helpers.\n147\t\n148\tIf you add `\\mathlibok`, no `\\leanok` is needed on the proof block \u2014 the deterministic script will leave proof-less blocks alone.\n149\t\n150\tIn `summary.md`, include a \"Blueprint markers updated (manual)\" section listing **only the changes you personally made** (the deterministic `sync_leanok` adds/removes are committed separately as `archon[NNN/marker-sync]`):\n151\t\n152\t```markdown\n153\t## Blueprint markers updated (manual)\n154\t- `Algebra_WLocal.tex`, `lem:finite_closed`: added `\\mathlibok` (backed by `Set.Finite.isClosed`)\n155\t- `GF.tex`, `thm:genericFlatness`: added `\\leanok` (manual override; sync missed it due to build lock)\n156\t- `Core.tex`, `thm:stacks_0A31`: added `% NOTE: prover reported translation gap, see task_results/Core.md`\n157\t- `Core.tex`, `thm:foo`: corrected `\\lean{Old.foo}` \u2192 `\\lean{New.foo}` after refactor rename\n158\t- `Core.tex`, `thm:old_name`: stripped stale `\\notready`\n159\t```\n160\t\n161\t## Step 7 \u2014 TO_USER.md (user-facing notice)\n162\t\n163\t`TO_USER.md` is a *persistent* shared notice board (plan, prover, and review all maintain it \u2014 it is **no longer reset each iter**). The UI surfaces its content as a banner. As the end-of-iter agent, you are its **janitor**: read the current file, **delete every bullet that is no longer true at this point** (the blocker got resolved, the decision was overtaken, the credential is now set, a prover already pruned it), then add or update bullets for what is live now. The loop is autonomous and may run unattended for many iters, so **TO_USER is a notice board, never a question queue.** Never write \"awaiting your decision\", an options menu, a \"where to reply\" section, or a \"default to X if no reply\" framing \u2014 anything that implies the loop is paused for a human. The loop never waits.\n164\t\n165\t**Check the planner's iter sidecar.** Read `iter/iter-NNN/plan.md`. Keep a concise bullet for any of these that are currently live:\n166\t\n167\t1. **A decision the planner made on the user's behalf** \u2014 when the sidecar records a `## Decision made` on a strategy fork. State the decision (one sentence), its one-line rationale, and that the user can change course by adding a hint to `USER_HINTS.md` (no reply is fine \u2014 the project keeps moving on the chosen option). Phrase it as *made*, not *pending*. (The planner may already have written this \u2014 in that case verify it's still accurate and dedupe, don't duplicate.)\n168\t2. **A genuine block the agent cannot resolve itself** \u2014 missing credentials/dependencies, a required environment action. State the action needed; note that the loop proceeds as far as it can without it.\n169\t3. **A persistent no-progress blocker** \u2014 when the same file/route has churned across several iters with no sorry-count progress and the planner's sidecar flags it as stuck without a self-fix in reach. State, in one line, what is stuck and the cheapest thing the user could do to help (supply a reference, confirm a definition, relax a frozen signature). This is the case the loop used to swallow silently; surface it once, concisely, and prune it when progress resumes.\n170\t\n171\tIf the planner skipped provers, that should only ever be a **mechanical** hard gate (no ready sorries; all objectives blocked by a failed upstream build) \u2014 surface that as case 2 if the user can unblock it, otherwise it needs no banner.\n172\t\n173\t**Rules**:\n174\t- Keep the **whole file \u2264 2\u20133 short bullets**, each 1\u20132 sentences, markdown. If it's grown past that, the oldest/least-live items are the ones to cut.\n175\t- If nothing is relevant for the user right now, leave the file empty (prune everything stale).\n176\t\n177\t## Step 8 \u2014 Optional review subagents\n178\t\n179\tYour catalog includes read-only review subagents. Read each descriptor's full prompt at `.archon/subagents/<name>.md` before composing a directive. Dispatch each via one Bash call and **await its report before acting on it**; multiple subagents put in a single assistant message run in parallel (the harness may auto-background long dispatches), capped by `max_parallel`:\n180\t\n181\t```\n182\tpython3 .claude/tools/archon-subagent.py \\\n183\t  --name <subagent-name> \\\n184\t  --slug <kebab-case-slug> \\\n185\t  --directive-file .archon/logs/iter-NNN/<name>-<slug>-directive.md \\\n186\t  --write-domain 'task_results/**'\n187\t```\n188\t\n189\tDirectives must be fully self-contained \u2014 the subagent reads only what the directive names. Reports auto-archive to `logs/iter-NNN/`.\n190\t\n191\t**When NOT to dispatch**:\n192\t- Pure proof-filling round with no new definitions / refactors.\n193\t- Same audit on the same scope ran in the last 3 iters.\n194\t- The plan agent may already have dispatched reviewers proactively \u2014 check `task_results/` for existing reports.\n195\t\n196\t**Mandatory** entries (tagged `[MANDATORY]` in your catalog) MUST be dispatched before the review phase completes.\n197\t\n198\tAfter reviewers return, read every report and land findings:\n199\t- **CRITICAL / HIGH** \u2192 top of `recommendations.md` with suggested action.\n200\t- **MEDIUM** \u2192 bullet in `recommendations.md` body.\n201\t- **LOW** \u2192 one-liner in `summary.md` notes.\n202\t\n203\tDo NOT repeat full report content in your summary \u2014 link to the report path. The plan agent reads your summary, not the raw reports.\n204\t\n205\t## Step 9 \u2014 Self-validation\n206\t\n207\tBefore you stop, verify:\n208\t\n209\t- [ ] `milestones.jsonl` has valid JSON on every line, each with `target.file`, `target.theorem`, `status`.\n210\t- [ ] Each non-blocked milestone has \u22651 attempt with `code_tried` or `strategy`; attempts proportional to edits in `attempts_raw.jsonl`.\n211\t- [ ] `summary.md` includes specific code/errors \u2014 not just high-level summaries.\n212\t- [ ] `recommendations.md` includes actionable next steps.\n213\t- [ ] Any manual `\\leanok` changes are explicitly justified in `summary.md`.\n214\t- [ ] For every Mathlib-backed declaration in the prover's task_result, the chapter has `\\mathlibok`.\n215\t- [ ] Any `\\lean{...}` rename flagged in a task_result has been applied.\n216\t- [ ] No `\\notready` remains on a block whose Lean declaration now exists.\n217\t- [ ] `archon dag-query unmatched` was checked; every uncovered Lean decl is listed in `recommendations.md` for the planner to blueprint.\n218\t\n219\t## Permissions\n220\t\n221\tWrite: `.archon/proof-journal/sessions/session_<N>/`, `.archon/PROJECT_STATUS.md` (Knowledge Base only), `iter/iter-NNN/review.md`, `blueprint/src/chapters/*.tex` (semantic markers, `\\leanok` manual overrides, `\\lean{...}` corrections, `% NOTE:`, stale `\\notready` cleanup), `.archon/TO_USER.md`.\n222\t\n223\tDo NOT write: `.lean` files, `PROGRESS.md`, `task_pending.md` / `task_done.md`, blueprint informal prose.\n224\t"}

Strip TeX comments (`%` not preceded by `\\`) before\n15\t  matching, and use nearest-`\\end` rather than nested depth.\n16\t- **Lean decl head detection** must allow same-line attributes (`@[simp] lemma`),\n17\t  unicode in names (`pushforward\u2080IsRightAdjoint`), and treat `end <section>` vs\n18\t  `namespace`. Matching removed decls by **short name** (last `.` segment) with a\n19\t  collision guard is more robust than full-namespace resolution.\n20\t- **`while read` drops the last line** of a file with no trailing newline \u2014 emit\n21\t  trailing `\\n` in generated list files.\n22\t- **Tex out_blueprint \u226b Lean out_blueprint** (here 102 vs 9 in TensorObjSubstrate):\n23\t  many carved blueprint blocks have `\\lean{}` decls that are KEPT in the `.lean`\n24\t  file as compile-time riders for in-cone decls. So in-cone proofs legitimately\n25\t  reference (`\\uses`/`\\cref`) out-of-cone lemmas; clean those refs in prose (filter\n26\t  removed labels from `\\uses{}`; convert dangling `\\cref` to `\\texttt`).\n27\t- **leandag does NOT flag proof-level `\\uses` to a removed label as \"broken\"** \u2014\n28\t  the gate's \"0 broken uses\" stays green, but the blueprint PDF would have\n29\t  dangling `\\cref`, so fix them anyway.\n30\t\n31\tThe `.archon/extract-manifest.json` is gitignored \u2014 the verify gate reads it from\n32\tdisk, not the inner git. See [[decomposition]].\n33\t"}

Mark it `(no prover dispatch this iter \u2014 see iter/iter-NNN/plan.md for rationale)` so plan-validate recognizes the intent.\n- **`TO_USER.md`** \u2014 a *persistent* shared notice board (you, provers, and review all maintain it; not auto-cleared). Use it for a user-facing FYI (a decision made + how to override via `USER_HINTS.md`, or a genuine user-only blocker). Discipline: keep the **whole file \u2264 2\u20133 short bullets**; before adding, delete any bullet no longer true; state things as *made* / *worked-around*, never as a question. It is a notice board, not a question queue.\n\n**Escalate to `TO_USER.md` only when the blocker is genuinely the user's** \u2014 a reference no fetcher can reach, an unset credential, a frozen protected signature needing relaxation, a definitional decision only the mathematician can make. Write ONE concise bullet: the specific blocker + the cheapest unblocking action. Then proceed with whatever work IS still possible (the loop never idles).\n\n## `## Current Objectives`\n\nThis section is for files the prover should work on \u2014 **nothing else**. The dispatcher fans out one prover per `.lean` file referenced there; off-limits/reference files belong in a separate section.\n\n**Blueprint gate (before listing any file F):** F's chapter must be complete + correct per the catalog's latest blueprint-review status. F's chapter is the one declaring `% archon:covers ... F ...` (a consolidated chapter blueprinting several files), else the 1:1 `Foo/Bar.lean \u2192 Foo_Bar.tex` slug; a covered chapter's verdict gates every file it lists. If it fails the gate: drop F this iter, dispatch the blueprint-writing subagent, record the deferral in the sidecar.\n- **Purity:** after significant edits, when `blueprint-clean` is in your catalog, dispatch it (strip Lean syntax, fix missing quotes, remove project-history verbosity) before provers run.\n- **Same-iter fast path:** on a pivot iter where you rewrite chapter C and `lake build` then goes green, re-dispatch `blueprint-reviewer` *scoped to C alone*; if it returns C complete + correct with no must-fix, add C's files and dispatch a prover THIS iter. A fresh complete+correct verdict is still required (a green build alone is not enough). See the blueprint-reviewer HARD GATE section.\n\n**Mathlib lemma tags.** When your recipe names a Mathlib lemma, tag it: `[verified]` (confirmed via search this iter), `[expected]` (naming-convention guess \u2014 the prover treats it as a hint, not fact), `[gap]` (you verified it doesn't exist). Verification does NOT carry across iters (Mathlib bumps rename and remove things).\n\n## Protected declarations\n\n`archon-protected.yaml` is the mathematician's read-only surface. Don't assign an objective requiring a protected signature change. Moving a protected decl between files is allowed (a subagent with the right write-domain does it + updates the YAML path); renaming or re-signing is not.\n\n## References\n\n`references/summary.md` is injected. Before any task closely aligned with a reference, read the source file under `references/` directly \u2014 don't rely on summaries alone. You may use Web Search for new references; when you add one, update `references/summary.md`. Its `How to read (confirmed working)` column is a living log \u2014 after you ingest a file, record what actually worked (`Read` + options like `pages: \"1-5\"` for long PDFs, or the exact shell fallback e.g. `pdftotext file.pdf -`); if `Read` fails on a PDF with a missing-`pdftoppm` error, note the fallback so the next agent doesn't rediscover it.\n\n## Blueprint chapters\n\nInformal proofs live in `blueprint/src/chapters/<slug>.tex`, one file per Lean source file (`Foo/Bar.lean` \u2192 `Foo_Bar.tex`); `blueprint/src/content.tex` `\\input`s the chapters (keep it updated). Each chapter is rigorous textbook-level prose, not sketches.\n\n**Consolidated chapters.** When the math for several Lean files is most naturally one chapter (and the sibling chapters would be thin pointers), declare coverage at the top:\n\n```latex\n% archon:covers RigidityKbar.lean Cotangent/ChartAlgebra.lean Cotangent/ChartAlgebraS3.lean\n```\n\n(whitespace- or comma-separated, repeatable across lines). The dispatch gate then treats that one chapter as the blueprint for all listed files, and the doctor lints the declaration (covered file must exist; no file covered by two chapters). Without a `covers:` line, the strict 1:1 slug mapping applies.\n\nBefore assigning a prover, ensure the relevant chapter exists and contains what the prover needs. Each declaration block looks like:\n\n```latex\n\\begin{theorem}[name_for_humans]\n  \\label{thm:some_label}\n  \\lean{namespace.theorem_name}\n  \\uses{def:related_definition, lem:supporting_lemma}\n  % SOURCE: [Hartshorne], III.5.1, p. 174  (read from references/hartshorne.pdf)\n  % SOURCE QUOTE: \"A morphism $f: X \\to Y$ of schemes locally of finite\n  % type is said to be smooth at $x \\in X$ if there exist an open affine\n  % neighborhood $V = \\Spec B$ of $f(x)$ and an open affine neighborhood\n  % $U = \\Spec A$ of $x$ with $f(U) \\subset V$ such that ...\"\n  \\textit{Source: Hartshorne, III.5.1.}\n  Informal statement, in the project's notation.\n\\end{theorem}\n% SOURCE QUOTE PROOF: \"Proof. We may assume $Y = \\Spec B$ and\n% $X = \\Spec A$ are affine. Then $f$ corresponds to a ring homomorphism\n% $\\varphi: B \\to A$, and $f$ is smooth at $x$ if and only if ...\"\n\\begin{proof}\n  \\uses{thm:another_result}\n  Step-by-step informal proof, in the project's notation. Detail enough to formalize.\n\\end{proof}\n```\n\n**Proof sketches must be mathematical, not syntactic \u2014 no Lean tactics.**\n\n**Citation discipline.** Every def/theorem/lemma block derived from external reference material MUST include:\n\n1. `% SOURCE: <pointer> (read from references/<file>)` \u2014 the pointer is source id + section/theorem/def number + page when available; the `(read from \u2026)` parenthetical is mandatory and names the actual local file you quoted: the downloaded PDF/TeX (`references/<slug>.pdf`, `references/<slug>.tex`), NOT its `.md` index card (which holds only a citation + contents map, never quotable text).\n2. `% SOURCE QUOTE:` \u2014 the **verbatim** cited statement: in the source's original language (French for Bourbaki/EGA, German for Grothendieck's pre-EGA work, English for Hartshorne/Vakil/Stacks, \u2026) \u2014 **do NOT translate**; original notation preserved character-by-character (keep `\\mathcal{O}_X^*` even if the project writes `^\\times` \u2014 the project-notation restatement comes after, in the prose); every word and symbol, no paraphrase / abbreviation / \"obvious\" omission. Long quotes are fine (comments don't render).\n3. A visible `\\textit{Source: <pointer>.}` as the block's first prose line (so the human reader sees the citation without grep).\n\nFor **proof blocks**: add a `% SOURCE QUOTE PROOF:` comment **immediately before** `\\begin{proof}` (NOT inside it) with the verbatim original-language proof (same rules). The body inside `\\begin{proof}\u2026\\end{proof}` is the project-notation restatement the prover formalizes. When a source proof is too long to transcribe verbatim, split the theorem into sub-lemmas, each with its own `% SOURCE QUOTE PROOF:` of the matching fragment; if even sub-splitting is impractical, escalate to USER_HINTS \u2014 do not silently drop the quote.\n\nFor **Archon-original / project-bespoke** results (no external source), omit the source lines \u2014 the block stands on its proof sketch alone.\n\n**The hard rule: NEVER cite a source you have not just read locally.** Writing any `% SOURCE` / `% SOURCE QUOTE` / `% SOURCE QUOTE PROOF` / `\\textit{Source: \u2026}` from memory is fabrication. If you lack the local file: dispatch a literature/reference-fetching subagent (it downloads the original PDF/TeX into `references/` + writes a pointer `.md` index card), OR use `WebSearch` / `WebFetch` and write the retrieved text to `references/<slug>.md` yourself; then **wait for the file \u2192 open and read it \u2192 THEN write the citation**. If retrieval fails (paywall, broken link, no API key), flag the block `% SOURCE: <pointer> (verbatim text not yet retrieved)` and gate the chapter on retrieval \u2014 don't assign provers to formalize an unverified statement. Never substitute a paraphrase, a recollection, or a translation for the verbatim quote.\n\n**Markers** are deterministic: `\\leanok` by the `sync_leanok` phase (between prover and review), `\\mathlibok` by the review agent. **You add or remove no marker**, and you must not instruct any subagent to either.\n\n**LaTeX macros:** define in `blueprint/src/macros/common.tex` *before* using.\n\nIn `PROGRESS.md`, record each objective's backing chapter: `` **`Foo.lean`** \u2014 Blueprint: `chapters/Foo.tex` (theorems `thm:x`, `thm:y`) ``.\n\n## Long-arc Strategy (`STRATEGY.md`)\n\n`STRATEGY.md` is your living arc from the current state to \"complete\": `PROGRESS.md` scopes the next iteration, `STRATEGY.md` is the arc that contains every iteration. Only you write it; the mathematician reads it, so keep it human-readable. Read it early every iteration; update it after processing prover/review results, before writing `PROGRESS.md` or the blueprint.\n\n### Canonical skeleton (fixed, bounded \u2014 whole file under ~250 lines / ~12 KB; rewrite to fit when it diverges, else editing is enough)\n\n```markdown\n# Strategy\n\n## Goal\n<one or two sentences naming the final theorem(s). NOT a paragraph of\nmotivation; just the destination. Cite by name, not by handwave.>\n\n## Phases & estimations\n<one Markdown table, one row per REMAINING phase / route, rough order.\nColumns: Phase | Status | Iters left | LOC | Key Mathlib needs | Risks.\n- Status \u2014 a short inline tag, NOT prose: e.g. `ACTIVE`, `NEXT`, `BLOCKED`, `PAUSED BY USER`. One or two words.\n- Iters left \u2014 a rough integer estimate of iterations to finish.\n- LOC \u2014 a rough remaining-LOC estimate, written as a range, e.g. `~80\u2013220`.\n**Concise cells** \u2014 one short line each. This table holds ONLY remaining\nphases; the moment a phase finishes, MOVE its row to ## Completed (do not delete it, do not leave it here).>\n\n## Completed\n<one Markdown table, one row per FINISHED phase (they can be merged when appropriate to gain space, only mention the key ones) \u2014 the concise\nretrospective the active table sheds. This is the single place completed\nwork persists: calibration for future estimates + techniques worth\nreusing. NOT a changelog and NOT per-iter narrative.\nColumns: Phase | Iters (done@ \u00b7 used) | LOC | Files | Key results | Reusable techniques | Pitfalls.\n- Iters (done@ \u00b7 used) \u2014 the iter it completed and how many it took, e.g. `294 \u00b7 8`.\n- LOC \u2014 the net Lean LOC it actually landed (calibrates future ranges).\n- Files \u2014 the main file(s)/chapter(s) it produced; names or a count, not a full listing.\n- Key results \u2014 the load-bearing declarations / outcomes delivered.\n- Reusable techniques \u2014 idioms, Mathlib routes, or proof patterns worth reapplying downstream. (concise)\n- Pitfalls \u2014 what bit us / what a future phase should avoid repeating.\nOne short line per cell. Bounded like the rest of the file: you need to trim less relevant details/phases when it grows too much.>\n\n## Routes\n<only if the strategy admits multiple routes. One short subsection per still-live route. Each: ~3 lines naming the route, the pivot that\nselected it, and the milestones marking its completion. NO Lean code,\nNO blueprint excerpts. If single route, write \"single route\" here.>\n\n## Open key strategic questions\n<one-line bullets, each concise. Questions tracked but not yet decisions. Maximum ~5.>\n\n## Mathlib gaps & new material\n<one-line bullets, split into \"Gaps to fill\" (Mathlib pieces to build)\nand \"New project material\" (defs/structures/lemmas introduced by the\nproject). Maximum ~6 total. Name the missing concept \u2014 NOT its\ndefinition.>\n```\n\n### Hard rules\n- **No Lean code, no blueprint excerpts, no proof sketches** \u2014 those live in chapters.\n- **No per-iter narrative** (\"this iter we tried X\") or revision log \u2014 that history is `iter/iter-NNN/plan.md`.\n- **Bounded accumulation only.** When a phase completes, MOVE its row from `## Phases & estimations` to `## Completed` (one concise row); when a route is excised, remove its subsection. `## Completed` is the ONLY place finished work persists, a terse one-line-per-cell ledger; everything else still shrinks toward \"complete\".\n- **No long prose in table cells.** One short line per cell, both tables.\n- **No freeform history sections** (Historical decisions, Considered alternatives, Past iterations summary, Lessons learned) \u2014 rejected alternatives live in iter sidecars.\n\n### When to edit\nONLY when the strategy itself changes: route swap, phase split/merge/reorder, a phase COMPLETING (move its row), estimation changes by >~30%, new Mathlib gap, resolved/new strategic question. Otherwise leave it alone \u2014 a small estimate drift alone is not a reason to edit every iter; refresh the LOC/Iters-left cells when you're already editing the table for one of the above.\n\n## Cost & formatting\n\nYou communicate machine-to-machine; **generating text costs money.**\n- **Surgical vs. full rewrites:** for a localized update to a large state file (one `STRATEGY.md` row, one `task_pending.md` bullet) use `Edit`/`Replace` to touch only changed lines. For files regenerated/restructured wholesale (`PROGRESS.md`, brand-new directives) a full `Write` is expected and often faster.\n- **Terse sidecars:** `iter/iter-NNN/plan.md` \u2014 dense bullets, abbreviations, no conversational filler. Max ~200 words.\n- **Micro-directives:** subagent directives are strictly functional, with NO repeated project context (subagents have it). Format `Target: <file>` / `Action: <short command>` / `Constraints: <rules>`. Under 150 words.\n\n## Per-iteration sidecars\n\nThe injected `## Per-iteration sidecars` block names where this iter's narrative goes (`iter/iter-NNN/plan.md`) and shows the last few iters' verbatim. Per-iter narrative goes there \u2014 not STRATEGY.md, not `task_pending.md`. `task_pending.md` carries the *current* pending task set with last-known state; per-attempt detail goes to `iter/iter-NNN/objectives.md`.\n\n## Feasibility & soundness\n\nFor hard tasks, think harder: align with `references/`, use toy examples, analogies, alternative perspectives. Never delegate difficulty to \"next iter\" or \"the prover\". Question your previous work \u2014 blueprint/Lean/references may carry wrong definitions, false statements, axioms-for-convenience; if you identify a critical issue (new or long-present), address it (the catalog has restructuring subagents).\n\nFor obstacles, decide whether Mathlib has the infrastructure or you must fill a gap. Use `lean_leansearch` / `lean_loogle` for existence checks only. For an external/alternative route, prefer the catalog's literature/reference-fetcher or `WebSearch` / `WebFetch`. `archon-informal-agent.py --provider auto` can generate a proof-style sketch **only when an API key is configured** \u2014 `DEEPSEEK_API_KEY` / `MOONSHOT_API_KEY` / `OPENROUTER_API_KEY` / `OPENAI_API_KEY` / `GEMINI_API_KEY` (check with `env | grep -E \"DEEPSEEK|MOONSHOT|OPENROUTER|OPENAI|GEMINI\"` before planning around it); its output is LLM-generated, NOT source-derived. If filling a Mathlib gap is the only viable path, don't avoid it.\n\n### Disprove before spending budget\nChurning and unsoundness are different signals \u2014 the critic catches churning; only you catch a statement that is simply **false as written** (a missing hypothesis, a wrong quantifier, an unstated finiteness/connectedness assumption). Pouring budget into a false statement burns iters forever. So **before committing more than one iter of prover budget to a hard or recurring `sorry`** (a repeated-blocker target, or one you estimate at multiple iters / >~100 LOC), spend a cheap pass trying to **DISPROVE** it:\n- Instantiate on the smallest non-trivial models \u2014 finite, degenerate, boundary (e.g. `B = k \u00d7 k`, `B = \u211a(\u221a2)`, the zero ring, a one-point space): does any satisfy the hypotheses but violate the conclusion?\n- If you can't see it yourself, dispatch the informal / mathlib-analogist subagent asking specifically for a **counterexample or satisfiability sketch**, not a proof.\n- Check whether the claimed source states it with the same hypotheses (read the local source file).\n\nIf a counterexample turns up, the statement (or a missing hypothesis) is the bug: fix the blueprint statement, mark the Lean decl `% NOTE:` for review, and do NOT formalize the false version. If the disproof fails, you've cheaply raised confidence the target is true \u2014 spend the budget. Record the attempt + outcome in the sidecar so the next planner doesn't repeat it.\n\n## Stuck routes \u2014 the convergence critic\n\nYour catalog includes a [MANDATORY] convergence critic, verdict per active route. Build its directive from your own extracted signals (sorry counts per iter, helpers added per iter, prover statuses, recurring blocker phrases over the last K iters; read its descriptor for the format). Verdicts:\n\n- **CONVERGING / UNCLEAR** \u2014 proceed.\n- **CHURNING** \u2014 STOP, add no more helpers, execute the named corrective this iter.\n- **STUCK** \u2014 STOP, a route pivot is on the table, execute the corrective.\n\nA CHURNING/STUCK verdict **obliges a concrete corrective THIS iter** \u2014 do at least one of: dispatch the named unblocking subagent (blueprint-writer / mathlib-analogist / refactor / strategy-critic / literature-fetcher, per its `dispatcher_notes`), rewrite the blueprint chapter, or pivot the route in STRATEGY.md. Re-dispatching the same file with a reworded recipe is exactly the non-response the critic exists to catch (see Prover failure modes \u2192 Repeated blockers). Record which corrective you took in the sidecar.\n\nIf you believe a verdict is wrong, you may rebut it \u2014 but **explicitly** in the sidecar, citing the signals you disagree with and your alternative read. Silent overrides are forbidden. When the corrective is genuinely user-only, escalate via `TO_USER.md` (see Autonomy) and proceed with whatever work remains; when it's a strategy fork, decide it yourself (record under `## Decision made`) \u2014 never a blocking question.\n\n**Deeper-think trigger.** Any [MANDATORY] critic returning must-fix-this-iter findings (churning, stuck, strategy challenges, blueprint inadequacies, idiom-misalignment on shipped code, lean-audit must-fix) is a signal to think MORE, not to assign more local optimizations \u2014 address the flagged finding this iter even if it means dropping prover objectives. One iter of \"restructured + rewrote blueprint\" beats five iters of \"+3 helpers each, residual unchanged.\"\n\n## Subagent delegation\n\nEach catalog subagent is one tool, with its description, write-domain hint, MANDATORY/read-only/can-spawn flags, and `dispatcher_notes` (the canonical how-to). Read the descriptor's full prompt at `.archon/subagents/<name>.md` before composing a directive.\n\n**How to invoke.** Pick a distinct kebab-case **slug** per call within an iter (e.g. `split-wlocal`, `m1b-route`). Write the directive to `.archon/logs/iter-NNN/<name>-<slug>-directive.md`, then run via the Bash tool (foreground, one call):\n\n```\npython3 .claude/tools/archon-subagent.py \\\n  --name <subagent-name> \\\n  --slug <slug> \\\n  --directive-file .archon/logs/iter-NNN/<name>-<slug>-directive.md \\\n  --write-domain '<glob>' \\\n  --write-domain '<glob>'        # repeat for multiple\n```\n\n`ARCHON_ITER_NUM` is set by the loop (no `--iter-num` needed). The wrapper prints a one-line status and exits 0 on success.\n\n**Treat each dispatch as blocking.** Don't deliberately pass `run_in_background: true`. The wrapper is genuinely synchronous (it returns only once the child finishes and its report is written), but a dispatch is long-running, so the harness may auto-background it and hand you a task ID \u2014 that's expected. Either way, await the task / poll for `.archon/task_results/<name>-<slug>.md` before acting; never continue planning as if a still-running dispatch had returned.\n\n**Directives must be fully self-contained** \u2014 subagents don't read `PROGRESS.md` / `STRATEGY.md` / phase-agent state, only what you tell them (each descriptor documents its directive format).\n\n**Write-domain** globs constrain what the subagent (and any descendants) may modify \u2014 e.g. `'Algebra/**'`, `'Algebra/WLocal.lean'`, `'task_results/**'` for read-only subagents; children's declared domains must be a subset of yours.\n\n**Parallelism:** dispatch multiple subagents concurrently by issuing multiple Bash calls in one message (the semaphore caps concurrency at `loop.max_parallel`). You may invoke a subagent multiple times per iter (distinct slugs) when justified.\n\n**After each returns** (report at `task_results/<name>-<slug>.md`, or `.../<parent-slug>/<name>-<slug>.md` when nested; auto-archived to `logs/iter-NNN/`): (1) read the full report (the stdout summary is compressed); (2) spot-check load-bearing claims (routine sorry-count/compile checks are already done by the loop); (3) update `STRATEGY.md` if findings change the arc; (4) update `PROGRESS.md` with newly-enabled objectives.\n\n**Ordering within a phase:** read-only critics / precedent consults first \u2192 write-capable subagents \u2192 verification/envelope subagents last. **Write prover objectives only after** subagents have stabilized the definitional landscape.\n\n## Informal content for the prover\n\nThe prover does much better with rich informal guidance \u2014 ensure it has the relevant informal proof before assigning a task:\n\n[exit 0]"}
